genres.name: Electronic
genres.description: Electronic music encompassing subgenres like techno, house, ambient, and experimental, featuring synthesizers, drum machines, and digital production.

genres.name: Indie Rock
genres.description: Independent and alternative rock music, often guitar-driven and experimental, spanning lo-fi, post-punk revival, and modern indie sounds.

genres.name: Ambient
genres.description: Atmospheric, mood-focused music emphasizing tone and texture over traditional structure, ideal for background listening and soundscapes.

genres.name: Pop
genres.description: Popular music with a focus on strong melodies, hooks, and accessible song structures across a wide range of substyles.

genres.name: Jazz
genres.description: Improvisation-driven music rooted in swing, blues, and complex harmonies, ranging from classic standards to modern fusion.

artists.name: Nova Echo
artists.bio: Nova Echo is a synth-driven electronic project known for cinematic concept albums that blend spacious ambient passages with pulse-heavy rhythms. Since the late 2000s, Nova Echo has released a string of narrative-focused studio albums exploring science fiction themes and deep-space travel.

artists.name: Solar Lines
artists.bio: Solar Lines creates warm, melodic electronic music that sits between ambient and downtempo. Layered analog synths, gentle guitar lines, and field recordings give each album a sense of traveling through sunlit cityscapes and distant coastlines.

artists.name: Midnight Parade
artists.bio: Midnight Parade is an indie rock band mixing driving guitars with shimmering synth textures. Their albums often revolve around nocturnal city life, blending danceable rhythms with introspective lyrics.

artists.name: Crystal Harbor
artists.bio: Crystal Harbor is an electronic duo known for lush, vocal-heavy productions that blend dream pop sensibilities with club-ready beats. Their work often features collaborations with vocalists and producers from across the globe.

artists.name: Harbor Lights
artists.bio: Harbor Lights is a jazz ensemble celebrated for intimate live recordings in small clubs and waterfront venues. Their performances focus on atmospheric standards, late-night improvisations, and stripped-back arrangements.

artists.name: Silver Atlas
artists.bio: Silver Atlas is a long-running indie rock project that has evolved from noisy 90s guitar anthems to layered, atmospheric records in the 2010s. The band is known for detailed discography spanning three decades.

artists.name: Luna Vale
artists.bio: Luna Vale is a vocalist and songwriter whose airy vocals bridge electronic, pop, and R&B. Alongside solo work, Luna is widely recognized for collaborations and duet projects with producers and bands across the electronic and pop spectrum.

artists.name: Golden Transit
artists.bio: Golden Transit is an indie rock trio blending jangly guitars with subtle synths and layered vocal harmonies, often drawing on themes of travel, distance, and memory.

artists.name: Neon Apparatus
artists.bio: Neon Apparatus is an electronic producer focused on dancefloor-oriented tracks that fuse techno, house, and electro with retro-futuristic aesthetics.

artists.name: Quiet Maps
artists.bio: Quiet Maps crafts minimalist ambient compositions built from piano, guitar loops, and processed field recordings, focusing on slow-moving soundscapes and meditative drones.

artists.name: Velvet Signal
artists.bio: Velvet Signal is an electronic-pop project known for glossy production, hook-heavy choruses, and a rotating cast of guest vocalists from the underground club scene.

artists.name: Orchard Hymns
artists.bio: Orchard Hymns is a modern jazz quartet blending traditional harmonies with subtle electronic elements and pastoral themes inspired by rural landscapes.

artists.name: City Lanterns
artists.bio: City Lanterns is an indie rock band whose music captures late-night urban moods, combining reverb-soaked guitars with spacious production and introspective lyrics.

albums.title: Signal to Stars
albums.description: Nova Echo’s debut full-length, a cinematic electronic concept album tracing the first human-made signal to leave the solar system.

albums.title: Gravity Wells
albums.description: A darker, more rhythm-heavy chapter in Nova Echo’s space saga, built around themes of orbital decay and gravitational pull.

albums.title: Infinite Array
albums.description: An expansive, long-form electronic odyssey that blends ambient movements with towering synth crescendos and intricate percussion.

albums.title: Parallax City
albums.description: A warm, downtempo journey through late-night streets and sunrise skylines, full of melodic synth lines and gentle guitar textures.

albums.title: Aphelion Sketches
albums.description: Solar Lines explores distant orbits and quiet moments in space on this tightly crafted, melody-rich electronic album.

albums.title: Neon Alleys
albums.description: The band’s breakout record, pairing jangly indie rock guitars with synth flourishes and tales of nocturnal city wanderings.

albums.title: Mirrors at Midnight
albums.description: A more polished and expansive Midnight Parade, blending driving rhythms with reflective lyrics about late-night self-discovery.

albums.title: Tidal Reflections
albums.description: Crystal Harbor’s debut, full of shimmering synth pads, coastal field recordings, and emotive vocal features.

albums.title: Glass Tides
albums.description: A lush, vocal-driven electronic album balancing dancefloor-ready tracks with glassy, introspective ballads.

albums.title: Harbor Frequencies
albums.description: Crystal Harbor’s most acclaimed release, weaving intricate production, rich harmonies, and expansive electronic soundscapes.

albums.title: Live at the Marina Club
albums.description: An intimate club recording capturing Harbor Lights in loose, conversational form with extended solos and hushed dynamics.

albums.title: Midnight on the Pier
albums.description: A late-night waterfront performance featuring spacious arrangements and breezy, understated improvisations.

albums.title: Maps for Satellites
albums.description: A noisy, guitar-forward 90s indie rock record that introduced Silver Atlas’s penchant for dense arrangements and melancholic hooks.

albums.title: Silver Atlas: Greatest Hits
albums.description: A career-spanning compilation collecting remastered favorites from Silver Atlas’s formative decade of releases.

albums.title: Midnight Cartography
albums.description: A more atmospheric, reverb-drenched Silver Atlas record, mapping emotional terrain with layered guitars and subtle electronics.

albums.title: Echoes of Dawn (with Nova Echo)
albums.description: A collaborative album pairing Luna Vale’s airy vocals with Nova Echo’s cinematic synth landscapes.

albums.title: Neon Duets
albums.description: A short collection of duet tracks featuring Luna Vale alongside various electronic producers and vocalists.

albums.title: City of Glass (with Crystal Harbor)
albums.description: An expansive collaboration with Crystal Harbor, blending glassy synth work, detailed beats, and Luna Vale’s evocative vocals.

albums.title: Station Stops
albums.description: A concise indie rock EP built around themes of travel, missed connections, and quiet moments between destinations.

albums.title: Side Streets
albums.description: A moody, guitar-led EP capturing late-night walks through quiet neighborhoods and glowing intersections.
